# Transcrição: 02-Ecomerce Bundling Example

- **Fonte:** 02-Ecomerce Bundling Example
- **Duração:** 05:15
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:03]

This is an example of a single product landing page for Ecom. So in this example, the goal for us was to increase the average order value. And the way we did that was simply by introducing bundled discounts. So, you know, you can play with price, you can play with different types of promotions and offers. But the most easiest way for single product Ecom stores to increase that average order value is to literally just sell more. Now, there are some products where you can't do that. But where you can and where there's scope to sell more, you know, even for example, we had an example with the jewelry store, often some people like the jewelry so much they'll buy it as a gift, but they'll buy it for themselves as well. And if you encourage that, you can increase your average order value. So in this example here, you know, this is not your typical product page for Ecom, because what we're trying to do is to focus in on selling the product through the values, the benefits, the emotional angle. So, you know, when we talk about the 5W avatar, we're also talking about working on that on your landing page as well. So the 5W avatar is not just about working on your ads to get them to convert. But this is also about trying to get your landing page lined up with your avatar as well. So, you know, through the page, we've got information, the product values, etc., etc. And as you go down, we're trying to build up the value and then introducing the price. So here, for example, you know, previously, for this store, it was just a single product page, it was a standard product page, we had the image, description, price, and kind of the options to add more. This simple change literally doubled the average order value, if not more. And all we've done here is to just offer them three options to buy the product. So option one is, you know, you can just buy the single light on its own. The second option is to buy two and actually make a significant saving, or you can buy three and make a higher saving. Now, from a fulfillment point of view, sending two of the products, especially the way that it's boxed and the weight is far more efficient than sending two separate products, if that makes sense. So, you know, when you package up two or three or more of these products, we're not paying more necessarily the way that it's done on packaging on, you know, the weight is negligible for something like this. You know, they're all factors that you have to take into place. So for some products, actually adding two or three into an order could be quite a significant cost as well. So think about this from the front end, but also think about this from a logistics point of view, and figure out your price, your margin, and how much you can afford to offer as that kind of bulk discount, and then play around with that on your profitability. So for example, here, if we double the conversion, but our profit dropped, then that's

## [03:03 - 05:15]

something, sorry, if we double double the OV and our conversion dropped, then that's something that we need to think about with our profitability, because if we are costing the business more by bundling more, so we may have a great return on ad spend, but our profitability is lower because you take cost of goods into account, and therefore your end margin and your profitability is therefore lower, then that doesn't work out as well. So use bundling where the where the unit economics work for you. So that's just that's that's the caveat that I'd put in place. If you can get past that and you can bundle it up, then think about the discounted and the stepped pricing for adding more. There was another store that we actually worked on where we just did a tiered discount. So if you buy one of the product, it was I think it was $30. If you buy two, you say, let me just get this right, I think it was you buy two, you say 5% by three, you say 10% by four, you say 15% all the way up to 25%. And we bumped up the average order value just by simply offering those gamified discounts. So customers often, you know, if we'd had a standard product landing page and presented this as the offer without really selling the value, not just in the landing page, but the ad, then we may not have got they got the higher AOV with the same in fact, we increase conversion rate as well. So like that's the perfect blend. So increasing conversion rate and upping your average order value make a huge difference on your scaling. So this is just an example of how you can think differently about increasing your average order value. So, you know, if we talk about pricing and playing around with pricing, it could be your first option, it could be an option which doesn't appeal to you, but certainly bundling and upselling is a great opportunity to increase your AOV and therefore allow you to scale a lot more profitably with your ad campaign.
